no_changes_rationale:
no_changes
status: needs_investigation
verification_command: bash -c 'set -e; for id in ddx-6bb51a52 ddx-bbdd7564 ddx-95ec5ed5 ddx-cb63cdfc ddx-743bc194 ddx-8835a765 ddx-6602559c; do ddx bead show "$id" >/dev/null || { echo "missing $id"; exit 1; }; done'

reason: Epic ddx-5d49b14e decisively fails the Step 0 size check — 6 ACs span unrelated
subsystems (TD authoring, backend interface refactor, new axon implementation, migration
tool, chaos-test integration, fallback documentation, performance benchmarking). Per the
execute-bead instructions, the correct action when a bead is too big is to decompose, not
attempt the work. A prior whole-epic attempt was BLOCKED in post-merge review (events log)
for producing only execution evidence and not addressing any AC.

The decomposition has already been performed in a prior attempt and the seven children
exist in the tracker with correct parent edges and dependency DAG (verified in this
attempt via `ddx bead show` and `ddx bead show <child>` for each):

  ddx-6bb51a52  TD: design axon-backend integration for bead tracker
  ddx-bbdd7564  Introduce selectable bead Backend interface in cli/internal/bead   (deps: ddx-6bb51a52)
  ddx-95ec5ed5  Implement axon Backend for bead tracker                            (deps: ddx-bbdd7564)
  ddx-cb63cdfc  Migration tool: .ddx/beads.jsonl + archive -> axon backend         (deps: ddx-95ec5ed5)
  ddx-743bc194  Run chaos_test.go against axon backend                             (deps: ddx-95ec5ed5)
  ddx-8835a765  Document bd/doltdb fallback backend wiring                         (deps: ddx-bbdd7564)
  ddx-6602559c  Bench: queue ops parity (axon vs JSONL) on 1100-bead archive      (deps: ddx-95ec5ed5)

This epic should remain open as the rollup; close it when all seven children land and
each passes post-merge review. Triage / orchestrator should now drain the children rather
than re-dispatching the epic. The lock-contention v1 patch (ddx-da11a34a) remains the
stability scaffolding while this plays out and is independent of this decomposition.

The verification_command above exits 0 iff all seven decomposition children still exist
in the tracker, which is the concrete, machine-checkable evidence that the decomposition
work is complete for this epic.
